{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44573", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-863", "name": "Incorrect Authorization"}], "description": "### Impact

Applications using the Pages Router with `i18n` configured and middleware/proxy-based authorization can allow unauthorized access to protected page data through locale-less `/_next/data/<buildId>/<page>.json` requests. In affected configurations, middleware does not run for the unprefixed data route, allowing an attacker to retrieve SSR JSON for protected pages without passing the intended authorization checks.

### Fix
The matcher logic was updated to perform the same match as it would on a non-i18n data route.

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade immediately, enforce authorization in the page's server-side data path instead of relying solely on middleware.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00606, "percentile": 0.45021}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-11T15:53:51Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44573"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-13T17:16:22Z", "published_at": "2026-05-11T15:53:51Z", "references": ["https://github.com/vercel/next.js/security/advisories/GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v15.5.16", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v16.2.5", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44573", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/vercel/next.js/security-advisories/GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/vercel/next.js", "summary": "Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in Pages Router applications using i18n", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-14T20:38:00Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-36qx-fr4f-26g5", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "15.5.16", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "next"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 12.2.0, < 15.5.16"}, {"first_patched_version": "16.2.5", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "next"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 16.0.0, < 16.2.5"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in Pages Router applications using i18n
